title: AC3 Bee Hunting Protocol for the Preparation of Sucrose Reward Solutions
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eu6bh","text":"This protocol describes the laboratory preparation of sucrose solutions (2 M and 0.5 M). It details the correct use of laboratory instruments, including a balance, hot plate with magnetic stirrer, graduated cylinder, and Erlenmeyer flask. The procedure ensures the accuracy of the final volume by adjusting to the mark in a graduated cylinder.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8rrqg","text":"Use distilled or deionized water for all solutions.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"47id0","text":"Ensure the balance is tared before each weighing.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ql8a","text":"Clean all glassware before and after use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dlvio","text":"Label all containers immediately after preparation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bvt4h","text":"Hot plate and heated water (80‑90 °C) can cause burns. Use heat‑resistant gloves.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a6hc1","text":"Wear safety goggles at all times to protect against splashes.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bfuet","text":"Handle glassware (graduated cylinder, Erlenmeyer flask) carefully to avoid breakage.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"brqf1","text":"Do not boil the water; heating to 80‑90 °C is sufficient.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

2 M solution: 1,369.2 g","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":18,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1bv8c","text":"For 2 M solution: n: 171.15 g","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":21,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6gpu1","text":"Distilled or deionized water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bskod","text":"Absorbent paper / paper towels","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6a97c","text":"Analytical or laboratory balance","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fg45f","text":"Weighing tray or weighing paper","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ddmj","text":"Hot plate with magnetic stirrer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fbmqr","text":"Graduated cylinder (capacity according to final volume)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4c96d","text":"Erlenmeyer flask or heat‑resistant beaker (capacity according to final volume)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6g88n","text":"Magnetic stir bar","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dcl8e","text":"Storage container with lid (capacity according to final volume)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"buk1s","text":"Heat‑resistant gloves","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"36td4","text":"Safety goggles","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Clean and organize the work area.
Verify that the balance and the hot plate are functioning correctly.
Weigh the sucrose:
Place a weighing tray on the balance platform.
Press the tare button (zero adjustment) so that the balance reads 0.000 g with the empty tray.
Carefully add sucrose until exactly 171.15 g is reached.
Clean and organize the work area.
Verify that the balance and the hot plate are functioning correctly.
Weigh the sucrose
Place a weighing tray on the balance platform.
Press the tare button (zero adjustment) so that the balance reads 0.000 g with the empty tray.
Carefully add sucrose until exactly 1,369.2 g is reached.
Prepare the initial volume of water:
Measure 1 L of distilled or deionized water using the graduated cylinder.

This initial volume (1 L) is smaller than the final volume (2 L) to facilitate the dissolution of the amount of sucrose (1,369.2 g). The total volume will be completed after adjusting to the mark in the graduated cylinder.
Transfer this liter of water to the Erlenmeyer flask or heat‑resistant beaker.
Heat the water and activate stirring:
Place the magnetic stir bar inside the container.
Turn on the hot plate and raise the water temperature to 80‑90 °C (boiling is not necessary).
Turn on the magnetic stirrer at low speed, gradually increasing the speed to maintain stable mixing.
Add the sucrose:
Add the weighed sucrose little by little into the hot water while stirring continuously.
Keep stirring until the sucrose is completely dissolved and the solution appears clear and homogeneous.
Transfer to the graduated cylinder and adjust the volume to the mark:
Pour all the solution from the flask into the 2 L graduated cylinder.
Adjust the final volume with distilled or deionized water until the lower meniscus coincides exactly with the 2 L mark.
Mix the final volume solution after adding remaining water to ensure homogenous mixing .
Store and label:
Transfer the final solution to a clean storage container with a secure lid.
Close the container and mix the final solution after adding the remaining water to ensure homogeneous mixing.
Label the container with the following information: concentration (2 M sucrose), preparation date, and the name of the person responsible for the preparation.
Prepare the initial volume of water:
Transfer this volume of water to the Erlenmeyer flask or heat‑resistant beaker.
Measure 500 mL of distilled or deionized water using the graduated cylinder.

This initial volume (500 mL) is smaller than the final volume (1 L) to facilitate the dissolution of the amount of sucrose (171.15 g). The total volume will be completed after adjusting to the mark in the graduated cylinder.
Heat the water and activate stirring:
Place the magnetic stir bar inside the container.
Turn on the hot plate and raise the water temperature to 80‑90 °C (boiling is not necessary).
Turn on the magnetic stirrer at low speed, gradually increasing the speed to maintain stable mixing.
Add the sucrose:
Add the weighed sucrose little by little into the hot water while stirring continuously.
Keep stirring until the sucrose is completely dissolved and the solution appears clear and homogeneous.
Transfer to the graduated cylinder and adjust to the mark:
Pour all the solution from the flask into the 1 L graduated cylinder.
Make up the solution to the final volume with distilled or deionized water until the lower meniscus coincides exactly with the 1 L mark.
Close the container and mix the final solution after adding the remaining water to ensure homogeneous mixing.
Store and label:
Transfer the final solution to a clean storage container with a secure lid.
Close the container and mix the final solution after adding the remaining water to ensure homogeneous mixing.
Label the container with the following information: concentration (0.5 M sucrose), preparation date, and the name of the person responsible for the preparation.
Store the final solution in a refrigerator. Keep the container closed and avoid direct sunlight.
Store the final solution in a refrigerator. Keep the container closed and avoid direct sunlight.
